# servicenow_ui_action (Resource)

`servicenow_ui_action` manages a UI action within ServiceNow. UI actions are buttons, links, and menu items that appear on forms and lists, allowing users to perform specific actions.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a UI action button on the incident form in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_ui_action" "example" {
  name         = "Example UI Action"
  table        = "incident"
  action_name  = "example_ui_action"
  active       = true
  form_button  = true
  show_insert  = true
  show_update  = true
  hint         = "Click to perform the example action."
  order        = 100
  script       = <<-EOT
    gs.addInfoMessage('Example UI action triggered for: ' + current.number);
    action.setRedirectURL(current);
  EOT
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the UI action.
- `table` (String) Name of the table this UI action applies to. Cannot be changed once created.

### Optional

- `action_name` (String) The action name used by the system to identify this UI action.
- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this UI action is enabled.
- `client` (Boolean) If true, this UI action runs client-side script when clicked.
- `comments` (String) Comments describing this UI action.
- `condition` (String) Condition under which this UI action is displayed.
- `form_button` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a button on the form.
- `form_button_v2` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a v2 form button.
- `form_context_menu` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears in the form context menu.
- `form_link` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a related link on the form.
- `form_menu_button_v2` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears in the v2 form menu button.
- `hint` (String) Tooltip hint text shown on hover.
- `list_action` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a list action.
- `list_banner_button` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a list banner button.
- `list_button` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a list button.
- `list_choice` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a list choice action.
- `list_context_menu` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears in the list context menu.
- `list_link` (Boolean) If true, this UI action appears as a related link on the list.
- `onclick` (String) Client-side onclick JavaScript handler.
- `order` (Number) The order in which this UI action appears relative to others.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `script` (String) Script executed when this UI action runs.
- `show_insert` (Boolean) If true, the UI action is visible when the record is being inserted.
- `show_multiple_update` (Boolean) If true, the UI action is visible during multiple update operations.
- `show_query` (Boolean) If true, the UI action is visible when querying records.
- `show_update` (Boolean) If true, the UI action is visible when the record is being updated.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_ui_action.example <sys_id>
```
